heading: Prohibitions regarding human fetal tissue
label: 289g–2 Prohibitions regarding human fetal tissue
notes: Amendments2006—Subsec. (c). , added subsec. (c). Former subsec. (c) redesignated (d).Pub. L. 109–242, § 2(2)Subsec. (d). , (3), redesignated subsec. (d) as (e) and substituted “section 289g–1(g)” for “section 289g–1(f)” in par. (1).Pub. L. 109–242, § 2(1)

children.heading: Purchase of tissue
children.label: a Purchase of tissue
children.text: It shall be unlawful for any person to knowingly acquire, receive, or otherwise transfer any human fetal tissue for valuable consideration if the transfer affects interstate commerce.

children.heading: Solicitation or acceptance of tissue as directed donation for use in transplantation
children.label: b Solicitation or acceptance of tissue as directed donation for use in transplantation

children.text: the donation will be or is made pursuant to a promise to the donating individual that the donated tissue will be transplanted into a recipient specified by such individual;
the donated tissue will be transplanted into a relative of the donating individual; or
the person who solicits or knowingly acquires, receives, or accepts the donation has provided valuable consideration for the costs associated with such abortion.

children.heading: Solicitation or acceptance of tissue from fetuses gestated for research purposes
children.label: c Solicitation or acceptance of tissue from fetuses gestated for research purposes

children.text: solicit or knowingly acquire, receive, or accept a donation of human fetal tissue knowing that a human pregnancy was deliberately initiated to provide such tissue; or
knowingly acquire, receive, or accept tissue or cells obtained from a human embryo or fetus that was gestated in the uterus of a nonhuman animal.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any person who violates subsection (a), (b), or (c) shall be fined in accordance with title 18, subject to paragraph (2), or imprisoned for not more than 10 years, or both.

children.heading: Penalties applicable to persons receiving consideration
children.label: 2 Penalties applicable to persons receiving consideration
children.text: With respect to the imposition of a fine under paragraph (1), if the person involved violates subsection (a) or (b)(3), a fine shall be imposed in an amount not less than twice the amount of the valuable consideration received.
The term “human fetal tissue” has the meaning given such term in .section 289g–1(g) of this title
The term “interstate commerce” has the meaning given such term in .section 321(b) of title 21
The term “valuable consideration” does not include reasonable payments associated with the transportation, implantation, processing, preservation, quality control, or storage of human fetal tissue.
